issue_title: Canvas.list_calendar_events should accept datetime instances
issue_body:
The `Canvas.list_calendar_events` method feeds its parameters down to a `GET /api/v1/calendar_events` request. [That request](https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/calendar_events.html#method.calendar_events_api.index) accepts `start_date` and `end_date` parameters, which “should be formatted as: yyyy-mm-dd or ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.” When using these parameters from Python, it would be convenient to provide values as Python `datetime` instances. For example, to list events between a course’ start and end dates:

```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
soon = datetime.today() + timedelta(weeks=2)
events = canvas.list_calendar_events(end_date=soon)
```

This seems like an obvious way to make the call, but iterating over the result fails:

```
canvasapi.exceptions.BadRequest: {'end_date': 'Invalid date or invalid datetime for end_date'}'}
```

The call works if I convert the date into a suitably-formatted (ISO 8601) string:

```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
soon = datetime.today() + timedelta(weeks=2)
soon_iso = soon.isoformat()
events = canvas.list_calendar_events(end_date=soon_iso)
```

It would be very convenient if `Canvas.list_calendar_events` could accept parameters in the standard `datetime` a Python programmer would naturally expect to use, and would handle the ISO 8601 conversion internally.

Of course, dates and times appear in many other places in the Canvas API. I am reporting this against `Canvas.list_calendar_events` because that is where it is affecting me right now. But perhaps the best fix would be at a lower level within `canvasapi` so that *every* `datetime` instance is converted using `.isoformat` whenever given as the value of any request parameter. Is there a single, centralized place where that could be done?

issue_title: Improper usage of `deepcopy` in `record`
issue_body:
`copy.deepcopy` does not always work with user-defined types, as described [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/10618956/copy-deepcopy-raises-typeerror-on-objects-with-self-defined-new-method).

So if a user-defined object is put into `record`, attempts to `deepcopy` the object would crash.

issue_title: Streamed decoding only provides unneccessarily huge chunks
issue_body:
When streaming encoding, it's working fine using small buffer-sizes (indeed only does so), thus presumably it should be able to decode in similarly small chunks, however the streaming decoder seems to only return 4.19mb chunks (i.e. i'm streaming data to BrotliDecompressBufferStreaming in 16kb chunks, but it only first returns anything whenever it has accumulated 4.19mb available). 

Given that this seems unneccessary (since the data was streamed on write in far smaller chunks), it would be very preferable to be able to have it return more frequent + smaller chunk sizes, as this kind of delay+chunkiness makes it unusable for some streaming scenarios, and less ideal for others.

issue_title: Support conforming filter syntax
issue_body:
The way `flask-rest-jsonapi` accepts filter params doesn't match the `JSONAPI` spec, and therefore makes it really ugly to filter with `JSONAPI` clients that support it. For example in Ember I have to do:

```
filter: `[${JSON.stringify({
  name: 'name',
  op: 'eq',
  val: name
})}]`
```

as opposed to what I can normally do with conforming server implementations:

```
filter: { name }
```
You can see why this might not be ideal.

issue_title: Make sure the LE package in doesn't break with rename
issue_body: We need to rename the client, which may mean renaming the packaged version(s) in PyPI. We should make sure this doesn't break things.

issue_title: Provide a command without any updates
issue_body: The "apt-get" calls in letsencrypt auto are error prone. Is there a way to call commands without updating and so on? The currenct letsencrypt-auto command does too much and produces too many errors to be used in standalone servers and production environments.

Recommended ssl configs include this. LE could generate that alongside the certs.

issue_title: Indicate what parameters are optional
issue_body:
## Feature Request
It's not clear to me from the pynwb docs what arguments are required and what arguments are optional. 

If I run:
```python
from pynwb import NWBFile
NWBFile()
```

I get the following error:
```python
TypeError: missing argument 'source', missing argument 'session_description', missing argument 'identifier', missing argument 'session_start_time'
```
which tells me which arguments are required. If I then run:
```python
from pynwb import NWBFile
from datetime import datetime
NWBFile(source='source', session_description='session_description',
        identifier='identifier', session_start_time=datetime.now())
```

no error, but what about the other arguments? The docs list many parameters:

> source (str) – the source of the data
session_description (str) – a description of the session where this data was generated
identifier (str) – a unique text identifier for the file
session_start_time (datetime or str) – the start time of the recording session
file_create_date (ndarray or list or tuple or Dataset or DataChunkIterator or DataIO or FORMDataset or datetime or str) – the time the file was created and subsequent modifications made
experimenter (str) – name of person who performed experiment
experiment_description (str) – general description of the experiment
session_id (str) – lab-specific ID for the session
institution (str) – institution(s) where experiment is performed
lab (str) – lab where experiment was performed
acquisition (list or tuple) – Raw TimeSeries objects belonging to this NWBFile
stimulus (list or tuple) – Stimulus TimeSeries objects belonging to this NWBFile
stimulus_template (list or tuple) – Stimulus template TimeSeries objects belonging to this NWBFile
epochs (Epochs) – Epoch objects belonging to this NWBFile
epoch_tags (tuple or list or set) – A sorted list of tags used across all epochs
modules (list or tuple) – ProcessingModule objects belonging to this NWBFile
ec_electrodes (ElectrodeTable or Iterable) – the ElectrodeTable that belongs to this NWBFile
ec_electrode_groups (Iterable) – the ElectrodeGroups that belong to this NWBFile
ic_electrodes (list or tuple) – IntracellularElectrodes that belong to this NWBFile
imaging_planes (list or tuple) – ImagingPlanes that belong to this NWBFile
ogen_sites (list or tuple) – OptogeneticStimulusSites that belong to this NWBFile
devices (list or tuple) – Device objects belonging to this NWBFile
subject (Subject) – subject metadata

As far as I can tell the only way to determine this is by trial and error. The input arguments are obscured by docval, so my standard PyCharm tricks don't work either.

There's a standard way to do this with the various docstring formats (Google, numpy, reStructuredText etc.), but we are using docval, so I don't really know how to handle it. Perhaps we could add the ability to mark arguments as optional, if it does not already exist?

- [x] Have you ensured the feature or change was not already [reported](https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/issues/) ?
- [x] Have you included a brief and descriptive title?
- [x] Have you included a clear description of the problem you are trying to solve?
- [x] Have you included a minimal code snippet that reproduces the issue you are encountering?

issue_title: fix missing FORM documentation link
issue_body: When form was moved to a subpackage of pynwb, this broke the links on the [API documentation](http://pynwb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api_docs.html). docs/source/api_docs.rst just needs to be updated to point to the right location of FORM

issue_title: argument `timeseries` of EpochTable takes type RegionSlicer
issue_body:
https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/blob/337d268178d89b070a241ad296edf929ac427bad/src/pynwb/epoch.py#L16

In all other cases, `electrical_series`, `spatial_series`, and `timeseries` arguments take the type of the same name. In this case, the `timeseries` argument expects a `RegionSlicer` object, which I think is confusing.

More broadly, if I want to create epochs, the most convenient way is to use `nwbfile.create_epoch`, however this does not allow me to create multiple `EpochTable`s nor to name the `EpochTable`s. If I want to name them, I think I need to use `EpochTable()` and `EpochTable.add_row`, however this requires me to do the `TimeSeries` slicing myself. I'm not that crazy about this slicing business in the first place, because, as I have said previously, it doesn't make sense to associate epochs with a single `TimeSeries` object.  Is there a way of defining multiple named EpochTable without doing the slicing myself? If not, could we create one? Maybe call it `EpochTable.add_epoch`.

issue_title: Tornado ioloop overhead
issue_body:
<!--
Welcome to the Jaeger project! 👋🎉

- Please search for existing issues to avoid creating duplicate bugs/feature requests.
- Please be respectful and considerate of others when commenting on issues.
- Please provide as much information as possible so we all understand the issue.
- If you only have a question, you may get a faster response by asking in
    - our chat room https://gitter.im/jaegertracing/Lobby, or
    - the forum https://groups.google.com/d/forum/jaeger-tracing
    (but please don't double post)
-->

## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?

We want the impact of tracing on request latency to be minimal

## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from solving the requirement?

It appears that traces add about 0.1-0.2ms of overhead per span (more if tags are added). This is surprisingly high. If a request contains 1000 spans, then at minimum we are adding 100-200ms of latency. 

I tested this by creating a method that just makes 10,000 spans in a loop. This function takes about 1.7 seconds to execute. cProfile shows that most of the time is spent waking up the other thread in tornado (`{method 'write' of 'file' objects}` is this line https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/blob/master/tornado/platform/posix.py#L54):

issue_title: [FBX] Embedded Texture in ASCII FBX doesn't seems to be read
glTF animation export: accessor count mismatch
issue_body:
When converting this test model: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/i71nlo1v63qre4v/AABAEtMwb_6nOpFC1bublffWa?dl=0
from FBX to glTF, there is a generated animation ("Take 001_17") which references accessors that have counts which do not align with the number of keyframes in the time accessor. 

In the included .glTF file, this can be seen in the accessors "Take 001_17_accessor_1" (scale) and "Take 001_17_accessor_2" (rotation) which only have a count of 1, while "Take 001_17_accessor" (time) and "Take 001_17_accessor_0" (translation) have 509 elements each. 

It seems like this problem only occurred with the "b_Root_$AssimpFbx$_Translation" node.

issue_title: BigQuery: ability to set default job / table / dataset configs in client class
issue_labels: [{'id': 82126549, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20bigquery', 'name': 'api: bigquery', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438582, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20feature%20request', 'name': 'type: feature request', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The [location](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/5148) is considered a global parameter (by the the BigQuery engineering team and the `bq` command-line tool), and we are tracking the feature to set a default location at https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/5148.
There are other non-global properties where it would be useful to set a user-configurable default.

For example:
- Labels on jobs. It could be useful to set default labels so that folks can track more easily which jobs are getting run by which application.
- `use_legacy_sql` on QueryJobConfig. Right now this defaults to standard SQL syntax, but some people might prefer to use legacy SQL for existing queries.
- The `destination_encryption_configuration` in jobs and the `encryption_configuration` in tables. Maybe this one actually makes sense as a global parameter, but it's not considered on in the `bq` command-line tool.

import json
from google.cloud import pubsub_v1
import google.auth
import time
from multiprocessing import Process, Queue
import os


os.environ["GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS"] = "/Users/ryan/Downloads/Multicoin Alpha-0b2b4e33e22d.json"
creds, project = google.auth.default()

def publish_messages(topic_name, messages, project="elegant-device-154517"):
    """Publishes multiple messages to a Pub/Sub topic. Messages as a list"""


    batch_settings = pubsub_v1.types.BatchSettings(
        max_bytes=1e7,  # ten megabytes (pub/sub max)
        max_latency=0.33,  # in seconds
        max_messages=1000
    )
    publisher_defined = False
    while not publisher_defined:
        try:
            publisher = pubsub_v1.PublisherClient(batch_settings, credentials=creds)
            publisher_defined = True
        except:
            time.sleep(0.5)

    topic_path = publisher.topic_path(project, topic_name)

    def publish(data):
        return publisher.publish(topic_path, data=data)

    encoded_messages = []
    for m in messages:
        data = json.dumps(m)
        # Data must be a bytestring
        data = data.encode('utf-8')
        encoded_messages.append(data)

    results = []
    for m in encoded_messages:
        results.append(publish(m))

    return results


class Example(object):
    def __init__(self, num):
        self.data = [{"test":"test1"} for n in range(1000)]
        self.messages = []
        self.successful_messages = []
        self.failed_messages = []

    def push_data(self):
        self.messages.extend(publish_messages("topic_name", self.data))

    def check_messages(self):
        for m in self.messages:
            if m.done():
                try:
                    self.successful_messages.append(int(m.result()))
                    self.messages.remove(m)
                except Exception:
                    self.failed_messages.append(m)
                    self.messages.remove(m)
        if len(self.messages) > 0:
            return False
        else:
            return True

    def push_and_check(self):
        self.push_data()
        total_messages = 0
        total_errors = 0
        complete = False
        while not complete: # while the messages have not all been verified to be sent correctly
            while not self.check_messages(): # while the messages haven't finished attempting to push
                time.sleep(.1)
            total_messages += len(self.failed_messages) + len(self.successful_messages)
            if len(self.failed_messages) == 0:
                complete = True
            else:
                total_errors += len(self.failed_messages)
                self.messages = []
                self.successful_messages = []
                self.failed_messages = []
                self.push_data()
        return total_messages

def worker(input, output):
    for func, args in iter(input.get, 'STOP'):
        result = job(func, args)
        output.put(result)
def job(func, args):
    result = func(*args)
    return result


import_queue, done_queue = Queue(), Queue()

test_num = 100000
print("building queue")
for num in range(test_num):
    import_queue.put((Example(num).push_and_check, []))

numprocesses = 5
print("starting processes")
p_count = 0
for i in range(numprocesses):
    p_count += 1
    Process(target=worker, args=(import_queue, done_queue)).start()
    print("started process #", p_count)

count = 0
for i in range(test_num):
    done_queue.get()
    count += 1
    if count % 10 == 0:
        print(count)
```

issue_title: Add AVRO as a valid BigQuery Source Format
issue_body:
The [BigQuery Documentation](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/loading-data#loading_avro_files) lists AVRO as a valid source format, but the [SourceFormat function](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/09061d1a1a97190bc3c4c4087bbdcd92c99e0e2e/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/job.py#L68-L73) in the client doesn't include it. Fixing this should be as easy as adding:

```
   AVRO = 'AVRO'
   ALLOWED = (CSV, DATASTORE_BACKUP, NEWLINE_DELIMITED_JSON, AVRO)
```

issue_title: Environment Name Can't Have Dots
issue_body:
See: http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/000095399/basedefs/xbd_chap08.html

Specifically:
> Environment variable names used by the utilities in the Shell and Utilities volume of IEEE Std 1003.1-2001 consist solely of uppercase letters, digits, and the '_' (underscore)

This means all dotted import names (like those used in the tests) must be replaced with an underscore.

issue_title: Make sure that column names are valid identifiers
Ordering in the rrule documentation keywords explanation
issue_body:
There is a difference in the order in which the arguments are listed in the function definition(at the beginning of the documentation), and the order in which they are explained. Specifically, cache is the last argument given when we are defining the function in the documentation, but is the first one to be explained.  (http://dateutil.readthedocs.io/en/stable/rrule.html). Another out-of-order keyword is byeaster.

Should we change the ordering in http://dateutil.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/dateutil/rrule.html#rrule to make it consistent?

issue_title: browsing local files doesn't save scroll position (i have a fix for this)
issue_body:
I've got over 700 artists on a local drive and I want to scroll through them to add songs to my queue (i've got the feature to add files to queue from browse)...

The problem is when I scroll down to say "The Rolling Stones", add a file to my queue, and go Back to the list of artists, it starts at the beginning. To get back to or past "The Rolling Stones" I have to flip through hundreds of artists again.

I think it would be nice if it scrolled to or stayed at the last artist you were browsing.

If the bug/enhancement is accepted I can put in a fix

My fix:
In library.getBrowseDir(), I append :scrollY=xxxx to the browse stack item if I'm going into a directory or I get the scrollY position off the popped stack item if I'm backing out.
In process_ws.processBrowseDir(), I call window.scrollTo(0,scrollY) if scrollY > 0.
I also clean up the browse stack item to make sure we query mopidy with :scrollY=xxx in there

issue_title: Slow _filter_existing_notebooks impacts each submission
issue_body:
On a deployment with ~600 students, _filter_existing_notebooks takes about 30s. This hits us when manual grading. A single submission is loaded (/formgrader/submissions/:submission_id) and that invokes api.get_notebook_submission_indices which calls the filter which walks the filesystem and filters out non-existing files. So when the grader presses Next, the next submission is loaded which causes another 30s filesystem walk.

### Operating system

### `nbgrader --version`
0.6.0.dev
### `jupyterhub --version` (if used with JupyterHub)
0.8.1
### `jupyter notebook --version` 
5.4.0
### Expected behavior
Indexes are cached or are built asynchronously?
### Actual behavior
Indexes are gathered for each submission load.
### Steps to reproduce the behavior
Manual grading in big course, visit assignment. Then visit first submission.

issue_title: GitHub Flavored Markdown incompatibility (tasklists)
issue_body:
In the [markdown example notebook](http://jupyter-notebook.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples/Notebook/Working%20With%20Markdown%20Cells.html), it says that the ["notebook webapp supports Github flavored markdown"](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/b8b66332e2023e83d2ee04f83d8814f567e01a4e/docs/source/examples/Notebook/Working%20With%20Markdown%20Cells.ipynb?short_path=6e5c622#L190). However, the [GitHub Flavored Markdown Spec](https://github.github.com/gfm/#task-list-items-extension-
) includes the tasklist extension, i.e. `- [ ]` should be rendered as a checkbox. But when I try this in Jupyter Notebook, it displays a bullet point followed by `[ ]`.  The markdown example notebook could be updated to reflect that the Jupyter Notebook is not fully compliant with the GFM spec, or support for task item lists could be added.

issue_title: save_notebook returns Promise sometimes, undefined sometimes
issue_body: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/static/notebook/js/notebook.js#L2229 returns undefined from a save_notebook call in the case where the notebook failed to load properly. All other returns from the same function return promises. It would be better if save_notebook returned an immediately rejected promise to avoid code in the caller that needs to first check if it got a promise at all before registering for success/failure callbacks on it.

issue_title: Issue Creating New Notebooks in IE only
issue_body:
I have an application that is framing user servers spawned by SystemUserSpawner. The notebook servers are configured to use no-tab mode in order to keep everything within the frame.

The issue arises when a user wants to create a new notebook. Upon selecting which kernel to use for their new notebook, the user is presented with a 404 page. This only occurs if the user is using Internet Explorer. Chrome and Firefox work perfectly, as does Microsoft Edge on Win10.

The logs and network traffic indicate that there is a GET request being fired on /user/<username>/undefined. This request obviously returns a 404 because undefined doesn't exist. After this call the normal, expected POST request to create the notebook is fired, and returns successfully; however, the 404 page doesn't re-route to the new URL. The network logs are as follows:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2753958/27398248-be9f8c70-567e-11e7-8423-c1af8cc820bd.png)

The relevant notebook server logs are: 
```
[I 2017-06-21 16:59:47.036 [username] log:47] 302 GET /user/[username] (172.17.0.1) 0.62ms
[I 2017-06-21 16:59:47.138 [username] log:47] 302 GET /user/[username] (10.45.141.204) 0.48ms
[W 2017-06-21 16:59:58.151 [username] log:47] 404 GET /user/[username]/undefined (10.45.141.204) 6.47ms referer=None
[I 2017-06-21 16:59:58.450 [username] handlers:170] Creating new notebook in
```

In both of these examples we can see the GET firing before creation. The odd thing to me is the fact that this initial GET request is _never_ called in Chrome or Firefox.

I've also ran IE tests outside of the frame in default tabbed mode and I get the same results. The new tab that is created displays the 404 page; however, sometimes the 404 page will re-route itself to the correct URL after the notebook is created.

I've dug around in the source code to attempt to find what might be causing this rogue GET request, but I was unable to pinpoint it.

Any help on this subject would be greatly appreciated. Thanks.

issue_title: TextDecoder not supported in IE/Edge
issue_body:
Currently, the TextDecoder API is not supported in IE and is in development in Edge: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/TextDecoder (see also https://caniuse.com/#feat=textencoder). This is used to decode binary messages at https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/179bb24fbf79d153812858126127a91431da3319/notebook/static/services/kernels/serialize.js#L20

We should probably put in a polyfill for IE/Edge, such as the one suggested by MDN: https://github.com/inexorabletash/text-encoding

issue_title: Support new (cloud-only) ability to suppress notifications on update
issue_body: In [JRA-34423](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRA-34423), Atlassian added the ability to suppress notifications on a per-update basis.  The implementation appears to be via a query string parameter (notifyUsers=false) on the update POST, per [the docs](https://docs.atlassian.com/jira/REST/cloud/#api/2/issue-editIssue).  It would be great if we could toggle that switch via the issue.update() method.

We generate these files upstream based on our internal knowledge of the AWS services. TemplateBody (string) --
Structure containing the template body. (For more information, go to Template Anatomy in the AWS CloudFormation User Guide.)

<type 'dict'>

Am I missing something or is the documentation due to be updated?

issue_title: How does Z3 handle SMT-LIB scripts with absent set-logic command ?
issue_body:
It has been my experience that Z3 is able to work glitch-free with SMT-LIB scripts, even when they do not sport an initial (set-logic) command, even though this is mandated by the standard.

For example the minimal script below yields (correctly of course) unsat:

~~~~
;;(set-logic QF_IDL)
(assert (< 2 1))
(check-sat)
~~~~

What does Z3 do in such cases (they might be much more complicated) ? Does it try to infer the minimal logic needed ? Or does it load the most general capacities (such as treating AUFNIRA) and goes from there ?

issue_title: ambiguities in README
issue_body:
Hi,
I share this because I tried to execute the install instructions contained in your README and faced two issues because of ambiguous instructions/small mistakes (or you are free to think that I'm too dumb :)):

- in the section title "Building Z3 using make and GCC/Clang" you suggest to change the `mk_make.py` line prepending `CXX=... CC=... python mk_make.py`; somehow, when first reading, I understood that I was supposed to change a line in the file `scripts/mk_make.py` to specify the content of the two variables; later I realized it couldn't work. Perhaps just a small rephrasing would help people not to fall in the same mistake

- in the very same section, you say that by default the `mk_make.py` looks for the `gcc` compiler; to my surprise I had it installed, but it was not happy with that, until I installed `g++` and run the `python mk_make.py` line again. Even though they are sometimes considered as "the same compiler", I think it's preferable to state that your tool looks for `g++` by default

Thanks for all your work.

issue_title: nrfutil dfu ble on Ubuntu 18.04 does not complete
issue_body:
I'm trying to verify DFU update capability in this configuration:
* Ubuntu 18.04 64-bit host
* nRF5-SDK 12.3.0 examples/dfu/bootloader_secure on target with S130 2.0.1
* nrfutil 3.5.1 with connectivity_2.0.1_115k2_with_s130_2.0.1.hex installed on the programmer

Invoking with:

    nrfutil dfu ble \
     -ic NRF51 \
     -pkg /tmp/app_dfu_package.zip \
     -p /dev/ttyUSB0 \
     -a E2:82:FE:1D:97:AC \
     -n DfuTarg

the debug output on the target shows the connection is made, and data starts being stored, but then the programmer terminates with:

```
  File "/home/pab/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nordicsemi/__main__.py", line 838, in ble
    dfu.dfu_send_images()
  File "/home/pab/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nordicsemi/dfu/dfu.py", line 129, in dfu_send_images
    self._dfu_send_image(self.manifest.application)
  File "/home/pab/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nordicsemi/dfu/dfu.py", line 102, in _dfu_send_image
    self.dfu_transport.send_firmware(data)
  File "/home/pab/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nordicsemi/dfu/dfu_transport_ble.py", line 549, in send_firmware
    response['crc'] = self.__stream_data(data=data, crc=response['crc'], offset=i)
  File "/home/pab/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nordicsemi/dfu/dfu_transport_ble.py", line 619, in __stream_data
    self.dfu_adapter.write_data_point(map(ord, to_transmit))
  File "/home/pab/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nordicsemi/dfu/dfu_transport_ble.py", line 332, in write_data_point
    self.adapter.write_cmd(self.conn_handle, DFUAdapter.DP_UUID, data)
  File "/home/pab/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pc_ble_driver_py/ble_adapter.py", line 366, in write_cmd
    raise e
pc_ble_driver_py.exceptions.NordicSemiException: Failed to ble_gattc_write. Error code: 3
```

Failure generally occurs after one or two, sometimes as many as 9, successful 256 By transfers.  The maximum I've been able to successfully transfer is 16 KiBy.  After failure I have to reset the target board to try again.

My hypothesis is that `3` is `NRF_ERROR_INTERNAL` and indicates a checksum error transferring data from the Linux host into the programmer.  I'm working to verify that, but hope somebody might provide further insight.

For completeness, I can't talk to the programmer at all when using PCA10031, PCA10028, or PCA10040 (with v5 connectivity), but I believe that to be either a CDC-ACM driver issue or a bug in `pc-ble-driver`.  In the steps above I'm using a Waveshare BLE400 which uses a more reliable serial driver on the Linux side.

issue_title: Install: Ubuntu 12.04 LTS python warning
issue_body:
User may see the following warning message during install and execution of `az`.

```
/usr/local/az/envs/default/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pip/pep425tags.py:30: RuntimeWarning: invalid Python installation: unable to open /usr/az/envs/default/lib/python2.7/config/Makefile (No such file or directory)
  warnings.warn("{0}".format(e), RuntimeWarning)
```

See also https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/1074.

We should investigate this to prevent the warning.

issue_title: [DevTestLabs] How do you create a lab?
issue_body:
### Description

Most (all?) of the commands underneath the `az lab` group require a lab name to be specified (which makes sense), but I don't actually see a way from the CLI to create a lab. Is this functionality missing? Is it expected that the lab creation is done via the portal, and then everything else can be done via the CLI? 

Apologies if I'm missing something super obvious here. I noticed that you can delete a lab, so I was expecting to see an `az lab create` command, but I didn't notice it.

---

### Environment summary

**Install Method:** How did you install the CLI? (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, nightly)  
Answer here: nightly

**CLI Version:** What version of the CLI and modules are installed? (Use `az --version`)  
Answer here: nightly

**OS Version:** What OS and version are you using?  
Answer here: macOS

**Shell Type:** What shell are you using? (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  
Answer here: bash

issue_title: "azdev test" no longer works with older profiles.

**To Reproduce**
run `azdev test` in a different profile than latest

issue_title: [help]: 'appservice web' command miss short summaries
OS Disk name for vm create is not stable -> command is not idempotent
issue_body:
From the vm_create function:

    ...
    os_disk_name = os_disk_name or 'osdisk_{}'.format(random_string(10))
    ....

This means that if I invoke the command twice with the same parameters (with os disk name omitted), I will (with a very high probability :)) end up with two disks, one of which is orphaned. This goes against the goal of having idempotent commands.

issue_title: "appservice web config ssl bind" for subdomain using wildcard SSL certificate is broken
issue_body:
### Current Behavior
**Given** an app service at myapp.mydomain.com
**And** an uploaded and active wildcard SSL cert for "*.mydomain.com"
**And** domain appservice object contains:

``` json
...
{
    "name": "app",
    "enabledHostNames": [
        "myapp.mydomain.com"
    ],
    "hostNameSslStates": [
        {
            "hostType": "Standard",
            "name": "myapp.mydomain.com",
            "sslState": "Disabled",
            "thumbprint": null,
            "toUpdate": null,
            "virtualIp": null
        }
    ]
}

```
**When** I send command:

```$ az appservice web config ssl bind --name myapp --resource-group mygroup --certificate-thumbprint thumbprint_of_wildcard_ssl_cert --ssl-type SNI ```

**Then** It returns (debug mode):

```
Response content:
msrest.http_logger : {"Code":"NotFound","Message":"Hostname '*.mydomain.com' does not exist.","Target":null,"Details":[{"Message":"Hostname '*.mydomain.com' does not exist."},{"Code":"NotFound"},{"ErrorEntity":{"Code":"NotFound","Message":"Hostname '*.mydomain.com' does not exist.","ExtendedCode":"04020","MessageTemplate":"Hostname '{0}' does not exist.","Parameters":["*.mydomain.com"],"InnerErrors":null}}],"Innererror":null}

Operation failed with status: 'Not Found'. Details: 404 Client Error: Not Found for url: https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/[sub_id]/resourceGroups/mygroup/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/myapp?api-version=2016-08-01
```
### Possible Cause

It appears the REST call payload generated by the CLI may not be properly populated:

Currently:
```json
{
    "properties": {
        "hostNameSslStates": [
            {
                "toUpdate": true,
                "sslState": "SniEnabled",
                "name": "*.mydomain.com",
                "thumbprint": "thumbprint_of_wildcard_ssl_cert"
            }
        ],
        "reserved": false,
        "scmSiteAlsoStopped": false,
        "microService": "false"
    },
    "location": "West US"
}
```
Should be (Tested and it succeeds):
```javascript
{
    "properties": {
        "hostNameSslStates": [
            {
                "toUpdate": true,
                "sslState": "SniEnabled",
                "name": "myapp.mydomain.com", // <-- need subdomain not "*"
                "thumbprint": "thumbprint_of_wildcard_ssl_cert"
            }
        ],
        "reserved": false,
        "scmSiteAlsoStopped": false,
        "microService": "false"
    },
    "location": "West US"
}
```

issue_title: KNACK Advisor module conversion
[Proposal] Support extension aliases
issue_body:
Add support for extension aliases so:
- extension names don't have to be as defined in the .whl filename
- extensions can be renamed

Refers mainly to `NAME` in `az extension add --name NAME`

There are ways around this such as providing the the appropriate name in setup.py and building a whl with the correct name.

issue_title: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'azure.storage' not always reproducable
issue_body:
### Which service(blob, file, queue) does this issue concern?

queue (possibly others?)

### What problem was encountered?
Installing `azure-storage-queue` (in a clean virtualenv) frequently (so not always) leads to a module installation which cannot be imported.
The installation steps followed are the ones documented in https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/queues/storage-python-how-to-use-queue-storage 

_In a fresh Python-3.6.3 virtualenv_

```
$ pip install azure-storage-queue
... installation happens successfully, no errors ...
$  python -c 'from azure.storage.queue import QueueService'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'azure.storage'

```
Sometimes the installation does result into a module which can be imported without errors but most often, ... it does not.

### Have you found a mitigation/solution?
A mitigation yes

`$ pip install azure-storage-queue --upgrade --force-reinstall
`
Fixes the problem. 
Keep in mind that the installation is done on a clean virtualenv.

I'd expect the import to succeed without any further ado after installing `azure-storage-queue`.
If specific details are required, pls let me know.

Tnx,

Jelle

issue_title: Automatically refresh service principal access token
issue_body:
The built-in authentication logic accepts service principal credentials to fetch an access token once. When this access token expires, the authentication logic does not refresh the access token.

The access token should be refreshed on-demand, if the existing token has expired.

This allows the user of the SDK to not worry about manually refreshing the token in the case of service principal auth, much like the existing support for refreshing user tokens.

Note: Special consideration may be needed re: how the service principal's credentials are stored for re-use when fetching a new token. Other Azure SDKs for Python (e.g., Azure SDK's auth library) may provide insight here.

# Internet (IP) protocols
#
# Updated from http://www.iana.org/assignments/protocol-numbers and other
# sources.
# New protocols will be added on request if they have been officially
# assigned by IANA and are not historical.
# If you need a huge list of used numbers please install the nmap package.

ip	0	IP		# internet protocol, pseudo protocol number
hopopt	0	HOPOPT		# IPv6 Hop-by-Hop Option [RFC1883]
```

Interestingly, the `ip` protocol isn't defined in https://www.iana.org/assignments/protocol-numbers/protocol-numbers.xml, and on my distribution, the content of `/etc/protocols` is generated by parsing that file directly.

issue_title: Head doesn't follow redirects by default but get does
issue_body:
## Long story short

The documentation says that the default behaviour of the library when fetching URLs is to follow redirects (up to the defined redirect maximum). This doesn't seem to be the case - it is true if the fetch is a GET but it is not true if the fetch is a HEAD.

This is important because it isn't clear in the documentation that HEAD behaves differently. If it should behave differently, the documentation needs improving. If HEAD should behave the same as GET with regards to following redirects, this is a bug.

## Expected behaviour

Trying to GET a URL that returns a redirect follows that redirect. Trying to HEAD that same URL should also follow the redirect.

## Actual behaviour

Unless `allow_redirects=True` is explicitly specified, HEAD returns the redirect status rather than the status of following the redirect.

## Steps to reproduce

    import aiohttp
    import asyncio


    async def fetch(session, url):
        async with session.head(url) as response:
            return response.status, response.url


    async def fetch_all(session, urls, loop):
        results = await asyncio.gather(
            *[fetch(session, url) for url in urls],
        )

        for ret in results:
            print("status: %s url: %s" % (ret[0], ret[1]))
        return results

    if __name__ == '__main__':
        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
        urls = ['http://www.linaro.org/this-doesnt-exist']
        with aiohttp.ClientSession(loop=loop) as session:
            loop.run_until_complete(fetch_all(session, urls, loop))

## Your environment

aiohttp (client) - 2.3.9
OS - Ubuntu 16.04
No proxy server

issue_title: Discard NTA_PLATFORM_* in favor of NTA_OS_* and NTA_ARCH_* macro variables
issue_body:
I think we could gain a lot of flexibilty by separate archicteture variables from operating systems variables and so discard combined platform variables (`NTA_PLATFORM_*`).

I suggest we have:
- Architecture variables: `NTA_ARCH_32`, `NTA_ARCH_64`, `NTA_ARCH_ARM` (@breznak already did partially this)
- OS variables: `NTA_OS_LINUX`, `NTA_OS_DARWIN`, `NTA_OS_WINDOWS`

This will simplify a lot the pre-processor work that I'm having with Windows build and probably in future PRs!

So instead of we use:

`#if defined(NTA_PLATFORM_win32) || defined(NTA_PLATFORM_win64)`

we could use:

`#if defined(NTA_OS_WINDOWS)` 

This will avoid a lot of file changes to review in Windows Build PR (#275)!!!

and instead of we use:

`#if defined(NTA_PLATFORM_linux32) || defined(NTA_PLATFORM_linux32arm) || defined(NTA_PLATFORM_darwin86) || defined(NTA_PLATFORM_win32)`

we could use:

`#if defined(NTA_ARCH_32)`

In only a few cases, we would have to use this, for example:

`#if defined(NTA_OS_LINUX) && defined(NTA_ARCH_32)`

**Note:**

I believe `NTA_OS_WINDOWS` is better than `NTA_OS_WIN` because 2 reasons: 

- `WIN` complicates a bit some regex expressions because `NTA_OS_DAR[WIN]` also contains `WIN`.
- `WINDOWS` is an extensible name just like `LINUX`, `DARWIN`. Otherwise you should see acronyms like `LNX`, `DWN`, etc...

Of course, in other contexts, it's well acceptable we use `WIN` acronym like `WinZip`, `winnt`, but in a context of extensible names `WINDOWS` is better..

issue_title: Possible bug in c++ CLAClassifierRegion
issue_body:
Hi,

This is against HEAD/ ec270f3918012304263db557d969c412bb705ea5

I've constructed a simple NontemporalClassification model, with a single input. Both the input and the output classifier are of type "string".

It's trained with:

```
for r in range(1000):
	ip = str( random_integers( low=0, high=99 ) );
	res = model.run( { "input": "A" + ip, "output": "Z" + ip } )
	print "A"+ip,  res.inferences["multiStepBestPredictions"][0]
```
The output is correctly of string form ("Zxx")

However, when classification is attempted:
```
	ip = str( random_integers( low=0, high=99 ) );
	res=model.run( { "input": "A" + str(ip), "output": None } )
	print  res.inferences["multiStepBestPredictions"][0], res
```
The returned category is a float.

When I change the CLAClassifierRegion implementation from cpp to py, I recover the expected behaviour of a restring response.

issue_title: Exit status is set to 0 on failure instead of 1
issue_body:
When running tests with nose and the flaky plugin, the exit status was not being set to 1 when the only tests that failed were ones that run with the flaky decorator and had max tries greater than 1.  If a test that was not decorated with `@flaky` also failed, then the exit status was properly set to 1.  

For example, here is a test that always fails, run with nose and without flaky.  The exit status is `1`.
```
$ nosetests test_flaky.py:TestFlaky
F
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1) FAIL: test_flaky_exit (test_flaky.TestFlaky)

   Traceback (most recent call last):
    test_flaky.py line 7 in test_flaky_exit
      self.assertTrue(False)
   AssertionError: False is not true


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1 test run in 0.0 seconds. 
1 FAILED (0 tests passed)
$ echo $?
1
```

... And here is the same test, run with nose and using flaky with the default 2 max tries.  It correctly runs the test twice and prints the correct results, but then exits with `0` status.
```
$ nosetests test_flaky.py:TestFlaky --with-flaky
-F
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1) FAIL: test_flaky_exit (test_flaky.TestFlaky)

   Traceback (most recent call last):
    test_flaky.py line 7 in test_flaky_exit
      self.assertTrue(False)
   AssertionError: False is not true


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
2 tests run in 0.0 seconds. 
1 FAILED, 1 skipped (0 tests passed)
$ echo $?
0
```

The root cause is that the flaky plugin was passes a newly created `TextTestResult` object to `_rerun_test` rather than the original object that nose created, leaving nose unaware of any rerun results.

issue_title: Allow multiple formats for DateTime deserialization
load None to special value, e.g. None -> []
issue_body:
I have many schemas which has some list fields, and I want all my list fields in my schema have a feature, that is load a `None` into `[]` automatically. So when I operate with advanced class object, I don't need to worry about it is a None.

This feature cannot be simply implemented by override `_deserialize`, because `_deserialize ` will not be called if input value is None.

Another way to implement this feature, is to add `@post_load` to each Schama, which needs lot of repeat code.

The only way to solve the problem, is to override `deserialize` in `List`, which is not the recommended way to subclass `Field`

'''Python
### Optional List & Set

# None, [] --load()-> []
# None, [] --dump()-> []

class SafeList(List):
    def _serialize(self, value, attr, obj):
        result = super(SafeList, self)._serialize(value, attr, obj)
        return [] if not result else result

    def deserialize(self, value, attr=None, data=None): # or override _validate_missing, None -load-> []
        result = super(SafeList, self).deserialize(value, attr=None, data=None)
        return set() if result is None else result
'''

issue_title: Make an extremely simple page for search.blockstack.org
Use simple duration values with units for input parameters to studioml
issue_body:
As an experimenter
I want to use time values of the form '2h4m10s' for durations specified by users
In order that confusion surrounding durations and units can be easily and consistently handled

Can be easily handled with code such as the following:
```
import re
from datetime import timedelta


regex = re.compile(r'((?P<hours>\d+?)h)?((?P<minutes>\d+?)m)?((?P<seconds>\d+?)s)?')


def parse_time(time_str):
    parts = regex.match(time_str)
    if not parts:
        return
    parts = parts.groupdict()
    time_params = {}
    for (name, param) in parts.iteritems():
        if param:
            time_params[name] = int(param)
    return timedelta(**time_params)

>>> parse_time('10s')
datetime.timedelta(0, 10)
>>> parse_time('12h5m10s')
datetime.timedelta(0, 43510)
```

issue_title: Can PyYAML be unpinned?
issue_body:
Hi guys,

I just updated my local Python version to 3.7 (which arrived this week in Arch Linux) and I'm migrating a series of packages to it locally, however I also depend on `kubernetes-py` which depends on a pinned version of `PyYAML`, `3.11`, which doesn't seem to work with Python 3.7 (it can't compile here at all).

If you use `3.13` instead, for example, it seems to work just fine (at least I managed to get it running here and doesn't seem to be causing problems). But I think that, as with any library, it's better idea to unpin the dependency, or, at max, establish version ranges if you want to avoid `4.x` for example.

Thanks,
Diogo

issue_title: Variables de patrimoine
Make entities __eq__ comparable
issue_body:
As soon as #326 got merged, having proper json generation we should implement the `__eq__` method in `Entity` mixin making entities fully comparable by equality of its fields.

Example:

```python
>>> Host(id=1) == Host(id=1)
True
```

The `__eq__` should serialize the entity to JSON, ensure it is `read` again from server and then compare all fields and values, only if all `_fields` has same values it should return True.

That helps with unit tests

issue_title: Make __eq__ compare all properties and create method "compare" to allow fine grained comparisons
Accept either hashes or IDs for foreign key fields
issue_body:
Entities may be related to each other via one to one or one to many relationships. For example, a product has a one to one relationship with an organization and a one to many relationship with repositories. In other words, a product may belong to one organization and may have many repositories.

The server typically describes these relationships using hashes of data. For example, if one reads a lifecycle environment by issuing an HTTP GET request to `/katello/api/v2/environments/:id`, the server will return a JSON payload in this form:

```json
{
    "name": "…",
    "description": "…",
    "organization": {"id": …, "name": "…"},
}
```

Similarly, one to many relationships are typically described like so:

```json
{
    "organizations": [
        {"id": …, "name": "…"},
        {"id": …, "name": "…"},
    ]
}
```

This is the typical way that relationships are described. But sometimes the server returns IDs instead. For example, if you issue an HTTP GET request to `/katello/api/v2/products/:id`, the server will return a JSON payload in this form:

```json
{
    "gpg_key_id": …,  # one to one
    "sync_plan_id": …,
    "organization": {"id": …, "name": "…"},
}
```

Notice how the one to one relationships on a product are represented using a mixture of IDs and hashes. There's no apparent rhyme or reason here. And one to many relationships may be represented like so:

```json
{
    "related_entity_ids": […, …, …],
}
```

NailGun currently deals with this weirdness by assuming that all relationships are described by hashes and adding in hacks as necessary whenever anything else is done. This makes NailGun less robust and is the cause of much repetitive code in module `nailgun.entities`. Rather than hacking around the hash/ID discrepancies on an individual basis, NailGun should search for both IDs and hashes when trying to populate foreign key fields on entities. Doing so will let NailGun be more robust and let us remove much redundant code from module `nailgun.entities`.

issue_title: Number of retries for a request
issue_body:
Bulk update using the bulk helper fails with the following error. I increased the timeout to 20 and that helped the bulk requests, but I still have some requests that fail with the following error. 

```elasticsearch.exceptions.ConnectionTimeout: ConnectionTimeout caused by - ReadTimeoutError(HTTPConnectionPool(host='localhost', port=9200): Read timed out. (read timeout=20))```

While I can increase the timeout, I was wondering if there is a field for setting `Number of Retries` within a request. I wasn't able to find this in the API and was wondering if there is a plan to have this implemented?

I understand that it may cause a problem with the version changing due to double update to the records that didn't fail within the bulk update. But it would be nice to have an option to enable retries.

issue_body:

issue_title: Support gzip for tile data
issue_body:
For example tippecanoe exports tiles to directory as uncompressed pbf, but normally (like for mb studio) you need gzipped pbf, otherwise mb studio rejects it. The mbtiles metadata unfortunately does not specify tile data compression, so you kind of need to know that the "pbf" usually gzipped pbf. It has no much point for png or jpg as these are already compressed, but for pbf it makes a lot of sense - 50 and more % reduction.

So if you use mb-util to convert folder with pbf tiles to mbtiles the resulting mbtiles is big and just does not work for some targets. So I suggest to add flag -gzip to the tool to compress each tile data in the 'images' table. As a workaround manual compression of tiles before running mb-util (something like `gzip *.pbf; mv *.pbf.gz *.pbf` in each folder) is also an option, but with larger tilesets (I have biggest ones with ~100M tiles) it is kind of tedious.

issue_title: WebComment: improve handling of long comments
issue_body:
Originally on 2012-09-20

Comments are stored in column *body* of table *cmtRECORDCOMMENT*, which is currently defined as *text*. This limits the length of each comment to max 65535 characters, which can be reached in several typical use cases (taking into account the HTML markup that could be part of the comment).

It should be considered if the column type should be increased to *mediumtext*.

Furthermore the business logic should catch any attempt at exceeding this limit to prevent any data loss: this could be done client-side to not let the user enter more that N characters, and/or server-side with an error message reported to the user upon submission. 
Alternatives could for eg. consider the creation of a second (or third, fourth, etc) comment in the table with the remaining text, marking this additional comment as part 2 (or 3, 4) in a newly added column (which would then be resolved to a single comment on the display).

issue_title: views: badges should contain "no-cache"-ing headers
issue_body:
This is part of a Travis badge SVG response headers:
```
$ curl https://travis-ci.org/zenodo/zenodo.svg\?branch\=master -L -I

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Mon, 28 Nov 2016 13:11:36 GMT
Content-Type: image/svg+xml
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Content-Type, Cache-Control, Expires, Etag, Last-Modified
Vary: Accept,Accept-Encoding
Cache-Control: no-cache
Etag: "a9e445ad2237440326816952c70dba79"
Pragma: no-cache
Expires: Mon, 28 Nov 2016 13:11:36 GMT
Content-Disposition: inline; filename="failing.svg"
Last-Modified: Thu, 24 Nov 2016 15:27:27 GMT
X-Content-Digest: 922c9cc746e8bc766b6acfed45d83e014df8a13e
Age: 0
X-Rack-Cache: miss, store
...
```

...while this is from an `invenio-formatter` badge:
```
$ curl https://zenodo.org/badge/6365411.svg -L -I

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Date: Mon, 28 Nov 2016 13:06:05 GMT
Content-Type: image/svg+xml
...
```

In badge responses we should include headers such as `Cache-Control`, `Etag`, `Pragma`, `Expires`, `Last-Modified` so they are correctly not cached. 

`Access-Control-Allow-*` headers (aka CORS headers) should be investigated as well (or at least documented).

*(Original issue from Zenodo: https://github.com/zenodo/zenodo/issues/912)*

issue_title: django tests.py is not handled properly
issue_body:
Issue in #88 introduced a minor bug.

Django creates "tests.py" as the default test file name when you run `./manage.py startapp` and now pre-commit-hooks doesn't handle it properly, seeking only files with test_*.py

Since the last fix added os.path.basename, we're always working with the filename, and should return to using test.*.py

Additionally - readme.md still references test*.py as well, and not test_*.py

The ultra-best-right way to handle this would be to handle it how python does it. unittest.TestLoader.discover does it by allowing any pattern, defaulting to test.py when no argument is provided.

https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/master/Lib/unittest/loader.py 
    def discover(self, start_dir, pattern='test*.py', top_level_dir=None):

issue_title: Investigate mypy version issues
issue_body:
When using an updated version of mypy (0.560), our mypy check passed on a particular commit when it should have failed (see #680).  As a result, we pinned the mypy version to 0.521 (see #689).  There is probably a mypy bug here that should be reported upstream, it'd be good to tell them about it.

Also, we should figure out which mypy version we should be using.  0.521 from 0.560 seems like a big jump backwards.  Maybe there's an intermediate (or later!) version that's better.

issue_title: Provide utility to generate transcript variants from HGVS-ish expressions with gene names
issue_body:
Unfortunately, some authors generate bogus HGVS expressions that use gene names rather than reference sequences. This issue should provide functionality to generate plausible expressions.

For example for TNFSF1A has 4 transcripts at the site of [rs104895271](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/projects/SNP/snp_ref.cgi?rs=104895271). So, for a gene-based variant like `TNFRSF1A:c.123T>C` return `NM_001065.3:c.123T>C`, and for `TNFRSF1A:n.426T>C` return `NR_144351.1:n.426T>C`. In general, there might be zero or more plausible variants for a given input. 

See code in `misc/experimental/hgvs-guess-plausible-transcripts`.

issue_title: pxd files missing in the PyPI package
issue_body:
In the package currently available on PyPI (v1.1.4), the two .pxd files (c_numpy.pxd and c_python.pxd) in pyregion/ are missing.
It is thus not possible to generate _region_filter.c directly from _region_filter.pyx using Cython.
Instead, the .c file itself is shipped.

Please ensure that for the next releases, the pxd files are shipped, and not the c file.

issue_title: Do I still need setup.py for *application* uploads to PyPI?
issue_body:
Note: `https://kenneth-reitz.uservoice.com/forums/913660-general` says "This site is not currently active" and I think this query is too speculative for StackOverflow, so I've posted the query here. (If there's an official answer, then it stops being speculative ;D )

From the (excellent) documentation, setup.py is really intended for Python libraries, packages that are imported by other packages. Obviously if I am building a Python library and uploading it to PyPI, I must create a `setup.py`. (In addition to my existing `pipfile` as required for `pipenv`.)

If I am building a local application, the documentation explains I don't need `setup.py` -- only `pipfile`.

But what if I am building an application and uploading it to PyPI? For example, so that a user can `pip install --user myapp` and then launch `myapp`. Do I still need `setup.py` in this case, or will the `pip install` succeed with just a `pipfile`?

issue_title: Function names in files
issue_body:
First off, I'd like to say THIS IS GREAT!

Im really excited about having a better version of requirements.txt.  I wasn't sure where the best place for suggestions on this project are, so I figured an issue at this early stage was a good place. As always this is just my initial thoughts, do what you will with them (that includes closing this issue).

1. The `dist()` name doesn't make a whole lot of sense to me. I suspect its supposed to be short for distribution. My suggestion would be to make it about what the user wants to do. ie `install`, `depends`, `require` . These are all names that are in similar use in other package managers and I think make more sense. But names are also hard.
2. I know a lot of confusion comes from what setup.py is for and what requirements.txt is for. It would probably be really helpful to have some guidance on that, and where this fits in there.
3. I heard cargo from rust as a nifty feature called features. It allows a package to declare features that you could enable and you would get a set of transitive dependencies. So Django could have a postgres feature that you could opt into. When you do the psycopg2 package would get installed. If you don't then you only get sqlite. This allows the Django package owner to specify which version of postgres is installed while not making the user install the world. This would probably require a bunch of changes to setup.py/everything-else, but having a list of features that you could enable in this API might pave the way for that.

Anyway, really looking forward to using this!

Thanks for listening

Mark

this us an alternative to #529 

instead of adding yet another feature to easy_install, packages would simply mark themselves as not compatible with the command, and need a pip call as installer

i strongly prefer packages opting out of egg installation over easy_install growing support for pip installation

issue_title: Concrete Inheritance: Reverse query name for 'model.field' clashes with reverse query name  for 'another_model.field'.
Problem: no easy ORM for CRUD/CRAB
issue_labels: [{'id': 430253576, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw0MzAyNTM1NzY=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/bigchaindb/bigchaindb-driver/labels/proposal', 'name': 'proposal', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}, {'id': 430255243, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw0MzAyNTUyNDM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/bigchaindb/bigchaindb-driver/labels/topic:%20UX%20docs%20cmd%20api%20driver', 'name': 'topic: UX docs cmd api driver', 'color': '1d76ff', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
* bigchaindb-driver version: 0.5.0
* bigchaindb **server** version (`bigchaindb -v`):
* Python version: 3
* Operating System: linux

### Description

Simple operations like
- create
- retrieve
- update
- burn

create and retrieve are quite easy to use, but update/transfer is very convoluted.
From the docs:
```python
txid = fulfilled_creation_tx['id']

asset_id = txid

transfer_asset = {
    'id': asset_id
}

output_index = 0
output = fulfilled_creation_tx['outputs'][output_index]

transfer_input = {
    'fulfillment': output['condition']['details'],
    'fulfills': {
        'output_index': output_index,
        'transaction_id': fulfilled_creation_tx['id']
    },
    'owners_before': output['public_keys']
}

prepared_transfer_tx = bdb.transactions.prepare(
    operation='TRANSFER',
    asset=transfer_asset,
    inputs=transfer_input,
    recipients=bob.public_key,
)

fulfilled_transfer_tx = bdb.transactions.fulfill(
    prepared_transfer_tx,
    private_keys=alice.private_key,
)

sent_transfer_tx = bdb.transactions.send_commit(fulfilled_transfer_tx)
```
There are many moving parts where things can go wrong. I would expect a simple ORM syntax will drive adoption.

The [`js-driver-orm`](https://github.com/bigchaindb/js-driver-orm) is a good starting point for this...

issue_title: output read coverage for all bases, even uncovered ones
issue_body:
Hi, 

I would like to create a numpy array with read coverage information for each bases of a whole chromosome, even uncovered region (depth = 0), to be able to retrieve a base coverage with the array indices.

I tried pysam's pileup function, but it seems to only output information for covered regions:

```
bamfile = pysam.AlignmentFile(bamFile, "rb" )
itr = samfile.pileup("chr22")
cov = np.fromiter([c.n for c in itr], int)
```

With samtools I can retrieve all bases coverage with: 
`samtools depth -aa *.bam`
or
` pysam.depth("-aa", "*.bam")` 

The pysam depth is clearly inefficient as I have to parse the output to get the coverage...

Is there a way to do it with pysam in an efficient way like with pileup function?

Thanks in advance for your help,
Maxime

issue_title: Overlapping paired-end read quality scores
issue_body:
When getting query base qualities out of a pileup, I'm often getting numbers that are way higher than I would expect qualities to be (>70). 

As far as I can tell, this happens when paired end reads overlap - in the overlap regions, quality looks like it's getting reported as the sum of the forward read and reverse read quality, whereas I would have expected to get the two qualities out individually.

Not entirely sure if this is intended behaviour or a bug.

Code to get base qualities out is below, this occurs using samtools 1.6 and pysam 0.14.1.

Thanks!

```
import pysam
samfile = pysam.AlignmentFile("ex1.bam", "rb" )
for pileupcolumn in samfile.pileup():
    for pileupread in pileupcolumn.pileups:
        if not pileupread.is_del and not pileupread.is_refskip:
            # query position is None if is_del or is_refskip is set.
            print(pileupread.alignment.query_qualities[pileupread.query_position])
            # Gives sum of forward + reverse read quality at that base in overlap locations?
samfile.close()
```

issue_title: Use "@" syntax to denote file name for all variable fields
issue_body:
`Job` launch and `Job Template` actions can accept a file name by starting the string with a `@` character, otherwise, it will take the variables given directly in the command line. Consider the following examples, where both commands pass the extra variable a=5 to the job launch, and a_equals_5.json is a file that contains the same text.

    tower-cli job launch --job-template=5 --extra-vars='@a_equals_5.json'
    tower-cli job launch --job-template=5 --extra-vars='{"a": 5}'

However, some other flags (for variables) can only specify a file. In other words, to set a=5, you have to create a file (such as a_equals_5.json) with that content and use the flag `--variables=@a_equals_5.json'`. These include:

- `--variables` flag on inventory
- `--variables` flag on group
- `--variables` flag on host
- `--source-vars` flag on group and inventory_source

There are several reasons the updated syntax is more usable. Saving a file often constitutes extra legwork and leaves junk lying around. It's also a quick route to passing local environment variables to something that resides in Tower. This would also be easy to implement in the next release.

issue_title: Handling of character set switching in UTF-8 mode
issue_body:
With pyte 0.6.0, the following code just leaves a B on the terminal display:
```
import pyte
screen = pyte.Screen(80, 24)
stream = pyte.Stream()
stream.attach(screen)
stream.feed('\x1b(B')
print(screen.display[0].strip())
```

Reading the code (and the associated FAQ entry), it says that the escape sequence should be ignored.
The code ignores it by not reading the last character.

However, if I run lynx in my shell (TERM=xterm, LANG=en_US.UTF-8), this sequence is sent quite a bit, leaving B in my terminal.

Leona  855
Caitlyn  27
Vladimir  26
Braum  23
Nocturne  22

Doing this a for a few other users quickly got my api key blacklisted. Did this really make over a thousand api calls? Is there a way I can get many many match histories in a few calls? or is every match always going to be a full api call? Right now I added another counter to stop after 50 match histories but this seems like a futile solution, since I am still making 50 calls for one users request. I don't really want to do a massive database system of match histories like how op.gg or other sites might do for match histories.

issue_title: Model.wait_for_action should handle action already completed
issue_body:
If [`Model.wait_for_action`](https://github.com/juju/python-libjuju/blob/master/juju/model.py#L867-L878) is called for an action that has already completed, it will block indefinitely.  If that's the case, it should handle it gracefully by returning immediately.  There may be some potential for a race condition here, so care should be taken to consider that.

If this is done, [`Model.get_action_output`](https://github.com/juju/python-libjuju/blob/master/juju/model.py#L1549-L1582) could be cleaned up to use it again.

issue_title: Add interface to set connection and read timeout
issue_body:
Some environment are more exposed to network congestion or JVM stalls, for example from full GC. However it costs more when the client abandons the query and submits it again, than to have longer timeouts at the socket level. Batch computations are usually less latency sensitive and more tolerant to higher timeouts.

`requests`allows to set the sockets connection and read timeouts. The `dbapi.Cursor` object should provide an interface to set them.

issue_title: Using bidirectional_rnn with lstm, returning sequences and with dropout
issue_body:
I am trying to code a bidirectional lstm. In keras this looks like this: 

    x = Bidirectional(LSTM(128, dropout=0.2, recurrent_dropout=0.2,return_sequences=True))(x)

following the example [here](https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/examples/nlp/bidirectional_lstm.py)  and the recommendations at #210 I started with this code

     net = bidirectional_rnn(net, BasicLSTMCell(128), BasicLSTMCell(128))

with the idea of  changing `BasicLSTMCell`, to `lstm`, given the fact that in the documentation, one can read: 

`"Build a bidirectional recurrent neural network, it requires 2 RNN Cells to process sequence in forward and backward order. Any RNN Cell can be used i.e. SimpleRNN, LSTM, GRU... with its own parameters. But the two cells number of units must match."` 

First, `BasicLSTMCell` does not have `dropout` parameter or `return_seq ` but `tflearn.layers.recurrent.lstm` does. However, `lstm` requires that you pass the incoming tensor, in this case `net`, so I thought about using this: 

    net = bidirectional_rnn(net, lstm(net, 128), lstm(net, 128))

but this returns an error because `lstm` has no `._n_units` attribute, and `bidirectional_rnn` needs that attribute. So, in summary: the cell I want to pass to the bidirectional layer is the following: 

    lstm(net, n_units=128, dropout=(0.2,0.2), return_seq=True)

Anyone can help?  

Thanks!

The logs show that `wal-e backup-list` is being invoked.

```
INFO: trying to bootstrap (without leader) 
create_replica_with_s3 start...

But somehow magically the `ret = subprocess.call` is finishing without resuming any paths in the script. I've put logs all around it and nothing gets called.

```python
    def create_replica_with_s3(self):
        logger.info('create_replica_with_s3 start...') # this gets printed

        try:

            return 2
        return ret
```

I've run out of basic python skills and google/stackoverflow ideas.

issue_title: Global Swagger Security Settings do not apply
issue_body:
### Description

according to the [Swagger 2 specs](https://swagger.io/docs/specification/2-0/authentication/), the following should apply:

> When used on the root level, security applies the specified security schemes globally to all API operations, unless overridden on the operation level.

This does not happen currently in Connexion. It is mandatory to define security on endpoint level to have security enabled.

### Expected behaviour

If `securityDefinitions` is defined in the swagger files, then it applies to all endpoints and secures them.

### Actual behaviour

`securityDefinitions` is defined in the swagger files globally, but no endpoint is secured.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a Swagger file with one endpoint and define `securityDefinitions` globally. test the endpoint with curl without any oauth information. a 401 should be returned, but the actual endpoint value is returned.

### Additional info:

I don't know if this is actually desired behaviour, but i would vote for following the Swagger rules on this one.

Output of the commands:

- `python --version` = `Python 3.6.2`
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"` = `Version: 1.2`

issue_title: Rating stars to use the numbers?
issue_body:
Considering FontAwesome is not using the numbers, could it include icons that actually NEED to be read as numbers? (e.g. by screen readers). Like Rating Stars! :-)

I invented this system with fonts years ago, to render stars from actual decimal numbers.
http://socialblogsitewebdesign.com/responsive-rating-stars/

You can even use 2.4/3 (which means 2.4 out of 3) to change the base outline to three stars, rather than the usual 5, or using different colors for the integer…

It doesn't use extra divs, images scripts or canvas. Only spans if you decide to add a forward slash with the base stars other than 5 (the default).

issue_title: Icon request: push-pin
issue_body:
### What can it be used for?

A push-pin is used in user interfaces to indicate that a collapsible panel should stay open. The existing thumbtack icon does not meet the requirements because we need a pair, one icon to depict the unpushed state and another to depict the pushed-in state.

### Icon request checklist

- [x] This is a single icon or matched pair (Ex: `lock` / `unlock`)
- [x] The title starts with "Icon request: " and is followed by the requested icon name (Ex: `Icon request: magical-unicorn`)
- [x] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate
- [ ] I have included at least one example image if it could be helpful (optional)
- [ ] The request is for a concrete object (it's harder to make an icon to represent happiness, it's easier to make a smiley face. ☺)

issue_title: Advanced `for_each` rule for input parameters
issue_body:
Currently the `for_each` rule will enumerate all possible combinations of input parameters. But there are cases we need additional constraints. For example certain routines should only be performed when N > P, etc. Have an additional constraint would be extremely helpful and implementation-wise I assume it is easy:

```python
x = [0,1,2]
y = [0,1,2]
z = [2,1,3]
rule = "_x!=_y and _y<_z and _z>2"
result = [(_x, _y, _z) for _x in x for _y in y for _z in z if eval(rule)]
# [(0, 1, 3), (0, 2, 3), (1, 0, 3), (1, 2, 3), (2, 0, 3), (2, 1, 3)]
```

But we need a good interface for it, and need to quit if `result` is empty or the rule has an error. 

I'm thinking how about when `for_each` is a tuple of length 2, it takes the parameters and the rule; and when it is a list it takes the parameters? eg:

```
for_each = (['x', 'y'], ('_x < _y'))
```

and 

```
for_each = ['x','y']
```
the original behavior. It's a bit ugly but we do not need a new parameter. Otherwise we can have and additional option `for_each_rule = '_x<_y'`. Would it be possible?

issue_title: server neutral workflow using CONFIG['localhost']
issue_body:
Right now the remote execution feature works like:

1. We are on a desktop (or server) with all the resources.
2. We write the script with the paths for this machine.
3. We define `path_map` etc so that local files could be synchronized to other servers.
4. We run the workflow mostly locally but farm some or all tasks to other servers. (e..g `sos run workflow -q cluster`)

This works well but the script depends on the local machine. I would recommend, thus document, the following way to make the workflow server neutral.

1. Define some directories that you think will be important in `config.yml`.
    ```
    localhost:
        project_dir:  /home/user/project
        resource_dir: /home/user/resource
    ```
    do this for all the servers that the script could be executed directly.

2. write your script as
    ```
    project_dir = CONFIG['localhost']['project_dir']
    resource_dir = 'CONFIG['localhost']['resource_dir']

    [10]
    depends: "${resource_dir}/hg19.fasta"
    ```

Now, if the `localhost` definition is made compatible with `path_map` of the server on other machines. The script would be

1. able to run on any machine without modification
2. able to run remotely on any machine without modification (with `path_map`)
3. If done properly, all signatures would be compatible and usable, regardless if the job is executed locally or remotely.

issue_title: Consequence of external removal of tracked files.
issue_body:
#543 raised the issue whether external `rm -rf` should trigger rerun. The current design is that the workflow would skip steps as long as file signatures are present, which is the case for `rm -rf` because that command does not remove signatures. The behavior can be confusing because some times steps seem to be mysteriously skipped.

If we are to trigger rerun with the removal of files, we would need to  change test for `target.exists` from `any` (namely `signature` or `target`) to `target`. This is almost trivial but raises the question as how to remove large intermediate files without rerun.

### Solution 1

Put signature next to file so that `rm -f file*` would remove `file` and `file.sig` and trigger rerun. In contrast, if a user remove a file without its signature `rm -f file`, the step would skip.

Pros:
1. seems to be easy to understand

Cons:
1. Contaminate file system with signature files.

### Solution 2
Use special command to remove file. For example, use command `sos remove file` to replace `file` with `file.sig` in user's file system.

Pros:
1. Less contamination of project directory because only files that are replaced with their signatures are different. 
2. This also allows users to remove `.sig` file outside of sos to trigger rerun.

Cons:
1. Special command to replace files with their signatures. 


### Solution 3
Use special signatures after using command `sos remove`.

Pros:
1. No contamination of project directory

Cons:
1. Confusing if no file is in project directory.
2. After the file is "removed", another special command is needed to really remove the file.

issue_title: List parameters and their annotations in `global` section with `-h` option
issue_body:
So suppose I have this workflow:

```
$ sos run workflows/fastqtl_to_mash.ipynb -h -v3


Available workflows
  export
  default
  convert

Sections
  convert_0:           Generate utility functions
  convert_1:           Convert summary stats gzip format to HDF5
  convert_2:           Merge single study data to multivariate data
  default_3:           Extract data to fit MASH model
  default_4:           Compute for MASH EZ model and save to RDS
  export:              Export notebook to HTML file
```

it neatly lists all steps in the workflow. But under `[global]` I have something like this:

```
parameter: cwd = path('./gtex6_workflow_output')
parameter: data_list = path("data/eQTL_summary_files.txt")
parameter: gene_list = path()
parameter: msg = "GTEx eQTL summary statistics"
# maximum number of groups per HDF5 file
parameter: maxsize = 1000 
parameter: cols = [8, 9, 7]
parameter: keep_ensg_version = 0
# number of null samples per gene to train MASH mixture
parameter: null_per_gene = 9 
# a list of effect names (SNP names) to exclude from mash analysis
parameter: exclude_mash_effects_list = 'NULL' 
# a list of condition names (tissue names) to include in mash output. 
# Default includes all conditions.
parameter: include_mash_condition_list = 'NULL' 
# size of MASH train set
parameter: mash_train_size = 20000 
```

Correct me if I am wrong, but I thought we can list parameters with `-v`? I do not think it works, though. It would be great if we can list parameters along with what they are, eg:

```
--exclude_mash_effects_list: a list of effect names (SNP names) to exclude from mash analysis
```

Not sure if `#` is the best identifier for parameter comments to show, but we can also do `##` or `#'` (`roxygen` convention). I'm just saying that showing parameter options could be useful when I make quick software-type of workflows for others to run without looking into the details.

issue_title: Update joblib-distributed documentation
issue_body:
In the joblib-distributed [documentation](http://dask-ml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/examples/joblib-distributed.html) 

`forest = ExtraTreesClassifier(n_estimators=1000,
                              max_features=128,
                              n_jobs=-1,
                              random_state=0)

# Build a forest and compute the pixel importances

t0 = time()
with joblib.parallel_backend('dask.distributed', scheduler_host=client.scheduler.address):
    forest.fit(X, y)

print("done in %0.3fs" % (time() - t0))
`
ExtraTreesClassifier is used as an example of using dask as a backend for joblib.  This feature will not work until scikit-learn v0.20.

The documentation should be updated to use something like scikit-learns gridsearch as an example; it will actually use the dask backend. See discussion in #231
